name: state-database
title: State Database with Poseidon SMT in Go for Enterprise zkEVM L2
hypothesis: A state database based on Sparse Merkle Tree with Poseidon hash implemented in Go can support 10,000+ accounts with state root computation < 50ms, compatible with witness generation for the ZK prover.
null_hypothesis: A Go SMT with Poseidon cannot achieve state root computation < 50ms at 10,000+ accounts, or the implementation introduces incompatibility with the BN254 field arithmetic required by the ZK prover, or memory usage exceeds 1GB at 10,000 accounts.
predictions: Go Poseidon hash will be 50-200x faster than JavaScript BigInt implementation due to native field arithmetic
Insert latency will be < 100us per account (vs 1.8ms in TypeScript RU-V1)
Proof generation will be O(depth) traversal, < 10us per proof
State root computation for 10,000 accounts via batch insert will complete in < 50ms
Memory usage will be < 500MB at 10,000 accounts with in-memory storage
